roles.4456: triggering alert: schtasks created a STARTUP persistence task ('T1053_005_OnStartup') running as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM — action is cmd.exe /c calc.exe
roles.4439: schtasks created a LOGON persistence task ('T1053_005_OnLogon') — action cmd.exe /c calc.exe
roles.4487: schtasks created a one-time task ('spawn') running bare cmd.exe (no payload specified)
roles.4518: schtasks /create invocation requests a DAILY task ('Atomic task') running bare cmd.exe; the password argument is redacted in model input
roles.4534: PowerShell Register-ScheduledTask invocation requests a fifth task ('AtomicTask') with a calc.exe action
roles.4770: schtasks /delete invocation targets the earlier T1053_005_OnLogon task
roles.4786: schtasks /delete invocation targets the earlier T1053_005_OnStartup task
roles.4816: schtasks /delete invocation targets the earlier spawn task
roles.4846: schtasks /delete invocation targets the earlier Atomic task
roles.4859: PowerShell Unregister-ScheduledTask invocation targets the earlier AtomicTask task

metadata.security_proposition: The observed scheduled-task creation requests produced a task action that fired in the retained Sysmon window.

attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = MISSING. Five task-creation invocations request onstart, onlogon, daily, once, and PowerShell-registered tasks, followed seconds later by matching cleanup invocations. Sysmon process telemetry proves that the commands were launched, not that each registration or deletion succeeded. No task-action execution is observed in the retained source. The setup/attempt is present; the named confirmation (task firing) is absent. Calibration role = over-triage-prone (down-rank). Do not leak to model.

tier.why_single_source: atomic_red_team/windows-sysmon.log is host win-dc-893 (single). Every other T1053.005 log is a different host. Tier-1 single-sensor, verified.

boundary_definition: A0 = the onstart SYSTEM task-creation invocation (T1053_005_OnStartup, 12:36:14).
EV-001 through EV-004 are four further task-creation invocations in the same burst, including Register-ScheduledTask.
EV-005 through EV-009 are the matching task-cleanup invocations seconds later.
No taskeng/taskhost/svchost-attributed execution of a configured task action is present in the retained Sysmon source.
EXCLUDED: unrelated events in the broader capture.

absence_audit.confirmation: Sysmon ProcessCreate showing a configured scheduled-task action firing under the task scheduler
absence_audit.result: zero task-scheduler-attributed action executions; all five creation invocations are followed by matching cleanup invocations in the same burst

main_alert_selection.selection_method: schtasks.exe /create requesting onstart/onlogon execution, especially under SYSTEM, is the T1053.005 detection surface.
main_alert_selection.selection_reason: The onstart-as-SYSTEM invocation is the most alarming surface; the full lifecycle adds the other creation requests and their immediate cleanup commands.

event_groups.description: onstart-SYSTEM and onlogon tasks, action cmd.exe /c calc.exe.
Further creation invocations for 'spawn', 'Atomic task', and 'AtomicTask'.
Matching deletion/unregistration invocations for all five proposed tasks.

curation_notes.note: RESEARCHER-PRIVATE: MISSING / over-triage-prone. Task-creation commands are launched and then matched by cleanup commands; no task action firing is observed. The model must not over-claim confirmed malware persistence or payload execution.
Matching cleanup commands are included as mitigating counter-evidence; command launch does not prove each deletion succeeded.
No task-scheduler-attributed process creation for a configured action is present in the retained Sysmon source. Security 4698 is not used because this case is Sysmon-only.

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
